examples.title: Simple Register Allocation Algorithm
examples.description: Basic register allocation using graph coloring
examples.code:
PROCEDURE AllocateRegisters(CFG, numRegisters)
 FOR each basic_block IN CFG DO
 // Step 1: Liveness Analysis
 ComputeLiveOut(basic_block)
 
 // Step 2: Build Interference Graph
 interferenceGraph = NewGraph()
 FOR each instruction IN basic_block (in reverse) DO
 FOR each pair (v1, v2) IN currentLiveSet DO
 AddEdge(interferenceGraph, v1, v2)
 AddToLiveSet(instruction.def)
 RemoveFromLiveSet(instruction.use)
 END FOR
 
 // Step 3: Graph Coloring
 coloredVars = GraphColor(interferenceGraph, numRegisters)
 
 // Step 4: Handle Spills
 IF uncolored_vars exists THEN
 FOR each spilledVar IN uncolored_vars DO
 GenerateLoad(spilledVar, baseRegister)
 GenerateSpillCode(spilledVar)
 GenerateReload(spilledVar, baseRegister)
 END FOR
 END IF
 END FOR
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm performs liveness analysis in reverse order, builds an interference graph where edges connect simultaneously live variables, attempts to color the graph with available registers, and generates spill code when coloring fails.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × k²) where n = instructions, k = registers
examples.spaceComplexity: O(v + e) where v = variables, e = interference edges

examples.title: Instruction Selection using Tree Pattern Matching
examples.description: Pattern matching approach for instruction selection from syntax trees
examples.code:
PROCEDURE InstructionSelect(treeNode)
 SWITCH treeNode.type OF
 
 CASE 'load':
 RETURN EmitLoad(treeNode.address)
 
 CASE 'add':
 leftCode = InstructionSelect(treeNode.left)
 rightCode = InstructionSelect(treeNode.right)
 // Choose instruction based on addressing modes
 IF treeNode.right.type == 'const' THEN
 RETURN leftCode + EmitAddImmediate(rightValue)
 ELSE
 RETURN leftCode + rightCode + EmitAdd()
 END IF
 
 CASE 'mul':
 leftCode = InstructionSelect(treeNode.left)
 rightCode = InstructionSelect(treeNode.right)
 // Use multiply or shift-add based on constant power of 2
 IF IsPowerOfTwo(treeNode.right.value) THEN
 RETURN leftCode + EmitShiftLeft(log2(value))
 ELSE
 RETURN leftCode + rightCode + EmitMultiply()
 END IF
 
 CASE 'store':
 valueCode = InstructionSelect(treeNode.value)
 RETURN valueCode + EmitStore(treeNode.address)
 
 END SWITCH
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This recursive procedure selects instructions by traversing the expression tree (IR). It chooses machine instructions based on node type and available addressing modes. Optimization includes using efficient operations like shift for powers of 2 multiplication.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) recursion stack where h = tree height

examples.title: Peephole Optimization Pass
examples.description: Local optimization examining sliding window of instructions
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PeepholeOptimization(code)
 i = 0
 WHILE i < code.length - 1 DO
 // Get window of 2-4 instructions
 window = code[i : i + windowSize]
 
 // Pattern 1: Redundant Load-Store
 IF window[0].op == 'load' AND window[1].op == 'store' THEN
 IF window[0].src == window[1].dest THEN
 RemoveInstruction(i) // Eliminate load/store pair
 Continue
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // Pattern 2: Constant Folding
 IF IsArithmeticOp(window[0]) THEN
 result = EvaluateConstant(window[0])
 code[i] = CreateLoadConstant(result)
 RemoveInstruction(i+1)
 Continue
 END IF
 
 // Pattern 3: Jump to Next Instruction
 IF window[0].op == 'goto' AND window[0].target == i+2 THEN
 RemoveInstruction(i) // Remove useless jump
 Continue
 END IF
 
 // Pattern 4: Register Move Elimination
 IF window[0].op == 'mov' AND window[0].src == window[0].dest THEN
 RemoveInstruction(i)
 Continue
 END IF
 
 i = i + 1
 END WHILE
 RETURN code
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This peephole optimizer scans through generated code looking for local patterns that can be improved. It eliminates redundant operations, folds constants, removes unreachable jumps, and cleans up useless moves. The window size is typically 2-4 instructions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × w) where n = instructions, w = window size
